sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
A week ago I started to get a cold. And, in the last two or three days, it seemed to move into my chest.
History of Present Illness
Logan is a 57-year-old male with a past medical history significant for coronary artery bypass grafting, hyperlipidemia, and ocd, who presents with an upper respiratory infection. Logan reports that about a week ago he started to get a cold, and in the last two or three days, it seemed to move into his chest. He had some post nasal drip for the beginning of the week and then it left his head, it went right down to his upper chest. He thought he had a slight fever, but no. He's been pretty good for the most part with the diet, but we did a little bit of travel over the last week or so, so the diet wasn't great. He's been tolerating the crestor 20 milligrams a day, no muscle aches. He had the bypass grafting last year and reports no chest pain. He's able to exercise before he got sick. He's vaccinated against covid. No one else in his house is sick as far as he knows. He's doing okay with his high cholesterol and we'll order another lipid panel for six months. If his symptoms worsen, he should call me.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
A physical exam revealed cervical lymphadenopathy. Lung exam showed bilateral ronchi and some wheezing. Oxygenation was 96%. The lipid panel shows cholesterol is generally good. The covid test is positive. There were no fevers or muscle aches noted.
Assessment
The patient presents with a confirmed covid infection and an upper respiratory infection. The vaccine has likely provided some protection, and symptoms are currently mild.
Plan
I want you to quarantine for 10 days, so try to avoid your other household family members. You'll have to take off work. Start supportive management. Order a chest radiograph. Continue the crestor 20 milligrams a day. Arrange follow-up in four weeks if your symptoms persist. Schedule a stress test in six months.
